exampleSentences: Sáng nay em dậy trễ nên chưa kịp chải tóc tai.
Anh ấy luôn chú ý chăm sóc tóc tai trước khi ra ngoài.
Chị thường đi cắt tóc tai mỗi hai tháng một lần.
notes:
tóc tai means hair, usually the hair on your head. It sounds more casual than just tóc, and often talks about someone’s hair in a general way.

It’s an uncountable noun — you don’t use classifiers with it. People often use it when commenting on someone’s appearance, especially if the hair looks messy or noticeable.

Compared to tóc (neutral), tóc tai feels a bit more expressive or slightly critical.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I woke up late so I haven't brushed my hair.
He always takes care of his hair before going out.
She usually gets her hair cut every two months.
notes_md:
**tóc tai** means hair, usually the hair on your head. It sounds more casual than just **tóc**, and often talks about someone’s hair in a general way.

It’s an uncountable noun — you don’t use classifiers with it. People often use it when commenting on someone’s appearance, especially if the hair looks messy or noticeable.

Compared to **tóc** (neutral), **tóc tai** feels a bit more expressive or slightly critical.
